name: transportation_policy_staffing_model_cedar_lib_eq
premise: A Transportation Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving transportation policy staffing model cedar lib eq. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as liberty vs equality under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Kaito Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Kaito Kowalski, accountable for a defensible call in Transportation Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays equality for liberty.

agents.name: Darius Kim
agents.identity: I am Darius Kim, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Transportation Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so equality isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of liberty.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect equality.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to equality to secure liberty under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Kaito Kowalski must decide under liberty vs equality. What does Kaito Kowalski do?
